evals.prompt: Make me a 6-slide presentation about the history of the internet
criteria: Produces exactly 6 slides
Includes a title slide
Uses sentence case headings throughout
Content slide bullets are concise (one idea per line)
Includes a closing slide
Output file is a valid self-contained HTML file
HTML file opens without errors and shows animations

evals.prompt: Create a pitch deck for a SaaS startup selling AI writing tools to marketing teams
criteria: Has a compelling title slide naming the product
Includes a problem or market opportunity slide
Uses at least one two-column slide for comparison or features
Includes a quote or social proof slide
Ends with a clear call-to-action closing slide
All text follows Apple Style Guide (active voice, no filler words)
Theme is dark by default

evals.prompt:
Turn this outline into slides:
- Introduction to machine learning
- Supervised vs unsupervised learning
- Real-world applications: healthcare, finance, retail
- Challenges: data quality, bias, compute cost
- Getting started: tools and resources
- Q&A
criteria: Maps each outline item to an appropriate slide
Does not invent facts beyond the outline
Uses appropriate slide types (content for lists, two-column for comparisons)
Adds an eyebrow label to section slides
Q&A becomes a closing slide, not a content slide
Output is a valid buildable JSON spec that the script accepts

evals.prompt: Make a 3-slide product demo presentation. Include a YouTube video on the second slide using https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ
criteria: Second slide is type 'media'
YouTube URL is correctly converted to embed format
Slide renders with an iframe element
Other two slides are title and closing
